Three chefs compete in a sandwich showdown! They make a spicy sandwich using just nine ingredients and build a stacked and jacked sandwich.
The team has twists on takeout and making delivery favourites at home. On the menu are orange peel chicken, souvlaki, and spicy pork lettuce wraps.
Four chefs hope for a sweet start. They find a green gummy shocker in their appetiser baskets, and race to the ice cream machine in the dessert round.
The bakers learn to craft bonbons at Jade Genin Chocolate. Then, the competitors seek inspiration for a dessert that uses three chocolate techniques.
The remaining bakers head to the farmers market. They make crepe cakes with fruit and incorporate unusual produce picks into their desserts.
The American bakers attempt to perfect the souffle. Then, they go big with a pâte à choux dough dessert that spotlights the egg in three ways.
Four chefs who proved their star status in the Tournament of Champions arena celebrate their first time on GGG with some classic games.
Guy Fieri looks for culinary creations you can sink your teeth into! He visits a catering truck full of beef tips, shrimp, and dirty bird wings.
Guy takes a walk down memory lane in Morehead City, North Carolina, when he stops at a place where the hushpuppies havent changed a bit.
Four Grand Champions are headed to the Chopped Kitchen for the holidays. The chefs are challenged with a kooky candy cane in the appetizer round.
Guy Fieris getting a taste of lights-out lasagna Bolognese in Naples, shrimp and grits in Tallahassee, and braised pork buns in Chicago.
Guy Fieri grabs fistfuls of flavour at a Mediterranean spot in California, a beachy bistro in New Jersey and an Argentine joint in Indianapolis.
Adam Sobel seeks to publicly humiliate Matt Jennings, and Marjorie Meek-Bradley calls out elite competitor Mei Lin.
The team shares comforting bar food recipes to recreate at home, including pub-favourite apps, cheesy burgers, and drink and snack pairings.
Guy Fieris setting his sights on seafood and creative comfort, including Szechuan biscuit sandwiches in Chicago and gangster gumbo in Florida.
In the appetizer round, a group of junior chefs cook with a classic Caribbean spice blend. Then, in the entree round, they must pair fish with fruit.
Molly Yeh makes shrimp fun rolls, sticky fried rice with lap cheong, fluffy ginger scallion chicken steamed buns and a matcha crepe cake.
Molly Yeh submits her chocolatey cookies to the harvest bake-off before making a hearty lunch of sandwiches, soup and tangy mac and cheese!

programmes.t: Be My Guest with Ina Garten
programmes.d: Ina Garten and Laura Linney enjoy cake for breakfast! Plus, they cook shrimp with a twist and make an unexpected find while antiques shopping.
In a burger-themed battle, the chefs get a deep-fried surprise and a sloppy classic in round one. Next, they must cook with a bizarre canned product.
Sherson makes Thai-style bouillabaisse and spiced grilled squid with tauchiu dip. Plus, Ann uses flower crabs in her Butter Crab.
Four kids tackle mystery baskets with a baking theme in an attempt to earn the third and final seat at the judges table.
Alex Guarnaschelli makes her triumphant return by taking on Rocco DiSpirito and Brian Malarkey. Guy Fieri hits them with his new swap wheel.
Guy checks out amazing Latin specialities from Peruvian sandwiches in Miami T Panama flavours in North Carolina to a joint in Arizona serving tacos and carne asada.
Guy digs into pizza with a surprise in Lansing, MI, standout Philly sandwiches in Wilmington, NC, and authentic Peruvian specialities in Tucson, AZ.
No body part is safe as Halloween hopefuls check in to the Hotel Henson! They make faces out of pie and impress the judges with blood spatter cakes.
Seven top kid chefs in the US travel to Orange Beach, Alabama, to compete in the first-ever World Food Championships Kids Grilling Invitational.
Home cook Sherria Watt rises to the challenge of a chocolate-themed bake-off against celebrity baker Duff Goldman.
Its sink or swim for home cook Lydia Wexler as she takes on the renowned Chef Michael Voltaggio in an intense salmon showdown.

The hosts celebrate the amazing mums in their lives with recipes inspired by them! The menu features rose elderflower sangria and savoury lamb sliders.
For Halloween, the chefs find spooky ingredients in their baskets. They struggle with a pasta brain and take their final shot with tasty syringes.
Valerie and Duff challenge the bakers to make cosmic cupcake pull-aparts. They must be decorated to look like the sun, a rocket, Saturn, a comet, or the moon.
Valerie Bertinelli and Duff Goldman challenge the young bakers to make loaded milkshakes topped with baked goods. Which bakers will secure a place in the final?
Valerie and Duff ask the final three bakers to make a two-tiered cake that captures the quintessential sights of an iconic city! Who will be crowned the winner?
Three chefs compete in a sandwich showdown! They make a spicy sandwich using just nine ingredients and build a stacked and jacked sandwich.

Guy Fieri visits a Ferndale, California, poke truck for some island-inspired flavours! Then, he has frosted pastries in a Palm Desert breakfast spot.
The chefs create a kitchen traffic jam when the first basket inspires them to use the deep fryer. Then, the competitors to must make juicy swordfish steaks.
Four social media influencers take to the stoves, where a sea bass fiasco in the entree round threatens to derail one competitors dish.
Eric Adjepong invites three competitors from Alex Guarnaschellis home city of New York to represent the citys diverse cuisine.
The hosts beat the winter blues with a taste bud vacation, featuring shrimp and chopped iceberg salad and Caribbean-inspired chicken stew.

A group of junior chefs must use a classic cornmeal dish in the appetiser round. In round two, the kids must combine a marshmallow treat with barbecue sauce.
The bakers create colourful doughnuts worthy of Indias Holi festival and elephant cakes inspired by Thailands Songkran Water Festival.
The bakers make cakes that have become decayed, using fermented ingredients like sauerkraut. The finalists create masterpieces that rot before the judges eyes!
